'''Bastila Shan's father''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] and was known to have been a treasure hunter. [[Bastila Shan|Bastila]] had a good relationship with him.

==Biography==
His wife, [[Helena Shan|Helena]], remembered their daughter with fondness, showing [[Hologram/Legends|holograms]] of her to one of her husband's employees. At some point during this time, Helena developed a fatal disease. Her husband, in order to pay for treatments, desperately continued his search for treasure to raise the money. In the course of these hunts he traveled to [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] searching for [[Krayt dragon pearl/Legends|krayt dragon pearls]]. Unfortunately for his cause, he was killed by a [[Canyon krayt dragon/Legends|canyon]] [[Dune Sea krayt dragon|krayt dragon]] in the [[Eastern Dune Sea]] sometime after the [[Mandalorian Wars]]. The dragon's nest was a [[Rakatan Ruins (Tatooine)|cave]] that housed a [[Rakata/Legends|Rakatan]] [[Star Map]].<ref name="KotOR" />

His [[Holocron/Legends|holocron]] with Bastila's holograms was eventually found by [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] and Bastila in the krayt dragon's cave while searching for the Star Maps, after [[Helena Shan]] revealed her longing for her husband's holograms.<ref name="KotOR" />

==Behind the scenes==
In-game, the image for the holocron within the inventory screen appears as though it were a Jedi holocron; however, its description characterizes it as a simple [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]]. Non-[[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitives]] could not create or use holocrons, but it is unknown whether this nomenclature is a mistake on [[BioWare]]'s part.
